title: Oliganthes discolor
intro: Oliganthes discolor, also known as 'Wild Strawberry', is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Apiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, providing potential health benefits in treating conditions such as arthritis and improving immune function. Bioactive constituents of Oliganthes discolor include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The plant is commonly used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to alleviate digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Oliganthes discolor is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Southeast Asia and is commonly found in tropical regions. The classification of Oliganthes discolor is further divided into the genus Oliganthes and the family Asteraceae, which is a part of the larger division of flowering plants called angiosperms. Within the family Asteraceae, Oliganthes discolor belongs to the subfamily Asteroideae. Further classification includes its order Asterales, class Magnoliopsida, and division Magnoliophyta.
